title: BitNet-TT session 9: Phase 2 — self_attn sub-op localization + cheap-lever tests
goal: Drill into self_attn, identify sub-op contribution, attempt kernel-level alignment.
outcome_summary: Sub-op captures show L0.post_attn_sub_norm RFE 0.420 as local max; L0.post_input_norm is near bit-identical (0.009). Three cheap fidelity levers (fp32 RMSNorm acc, SDPA HiFi4, manual RoPE) all produced noise-level or negative PCC deltas. Deeper SDPA algorithmic rewrite needed for alignment — out of cheap-lever scope.

stories.title: Sub-op captures in MultiHeadAttention
stories.notes: DONE. 5 capture points added inside _forward_prefill_with_preallocated_cache (post_qkv_{q,k,v}, post_rope_{q,k}, post_sdpa, post_attn_sub_norm, post_o_proj). Lazy _mc_attn import avoids circular import with transformer.py.

stories.title: Fine RFE ranking
stories.notes: DONE. L0.post_input_norm 0.009 (near-bit-identical), L0.post_attn_sub_norm 0.420 (local max), L0.post_self_attn 0.240, L0.block_output 0.139. SDPA/RoPE/sub_norm chain dominates drift at L0.

stories.title: Align top-1 sub-op kernel
stories.notes: ATTEMPTED + FALSIFIED. Cheap levers: (1) SDPA HiFi4 compute_kernel_config via BITNET_SDPA_HIFI4 env: PCC 0.981527 (-0.0005); (2) use_fused_rope=False manual RoPE: PCC 0.979598 (-0.0007); (3) fp32 RMSNorm accumulator (US-705): +0.0005. All regressions or noise. Conclusion: SDPA algorithmic divergence from PyTorch bf16 cannot be closed by compute_kernel_config alone. A true alignment would require rewriting SDPA to match PyTorch's reduction order/softmax approximation bit-for-bit — multi-day tt-metal kernel work, out of scope for this session.

stories.title: Joint re-measurement + commit
stories.notes: DONE. Current HEAD 9524369: decode_tps 71.05 t/s unchanged (flags all default OFF), PCC 0.982032 baseline. Phase 2 closes with a falsified cheap-lever hypothesis; next Ralph session would need to either (a) commit to a multi-day SDPA kernel rewrite, or (b) accept current 0.982 as the practical ceiling. User direction required before starting (a).
